name: Blood & Guts
creator: Dorian Yates
summary: Dorian Yates' brand of high-intensity training (HIT): after a few progressive warm-ups, you take one all-out working set per exercise to absolute muscular failure and then beyond it with an intensity technique. Low volume, low frequency, maximal effort, and full recovery drive the growth.
principles: Quality over quantity: one all-out working set beats many submaximal sets.
Train each muscle to complete failure, then push past it.
Hit every muscle group only once every 5-7 days for full recovery.
Warm up progressively, then commit fully to the single working set.
Track every set and add weight or reps over time (progressive overload).
Strict form and constant tension - use the weight as a tool, not an ego lift.

guidelines.warmupProtocol: 1-3 progressive warm-up sets pyramiding up to the working weight; never taken near failure.
guidelines.workingSetProtocol: 1-2 all-out working sets per exercise taken to failure, finished with forced reps, rest-pause, negatives, drops, or partials.

sources.title: Dorian Yates Workout: Complete Training Guide - The Barbell
